route_options: 20.5 km time trial, Praia do Pedrogao (cancelled-2026 plan, unconfirmed for 2027)

climate.description: Central Portugal's coastal-influenced Leiria region; specific seasonal conditions depend on the eventual 2027 date, not yet announced.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 67. NOTE (2026-07-24, editorial wave 5): dimension scores (organization, field_depth, value, expenses, etc.) predate this wave's discovery that zero Leiria-branded editions have actually been held — they were inherited unchanged from the migrated Coimbra-lineage baseline, not scored against a real Leiria event. Not recalculated this wave per the 'don't reshuffle numbers' rule; flagging for a human call on whether these should be nulled/marked provisional until a 2027 edition actually runs.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — no edition of this event has actually taken place. Granfondo Leiria Region – Sicó Lands is the UCI Gran Fondo World Series' new name and host territory for the event previously run as Granfondo Coimbra Region; the announced 2026 debut (a two-day format: a flat 20.5 km time trial at Praia do Pedrógão on the Saturday, then a 146 km/1,478 m granfondo and a shorter 93 km/1,000 m mediofondo starting and finishing in Pombal on the Sunday, heading into the Serra de Sicó limestone hills) was cancelled outright and postponed to spring 2027 after Storm Kristin caused widespread regional damage. The figures above are the organizer's officially announced 2026 course plan, retained on their cancellation notice — they have not been reconfirmed for the eventual 2027 edition, and route/distance specifics could change.
course_description.signature_challenge: Planned but unrun as of this writing: the cancelled 2026 course plan routed the granfondo into the Serra de Sicó limestone hills toward the region's published 1,478 m elevation total — no confirmed source details exact climb geography or how gain was distributed along the route.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Pombal and Leiria both have lodging options; confirm against the eventual 2027 date before booking, since no 2026 edition ran.
logistics.transport: Planned (but cancelled) 2026 format was a two-day UCI qualifier: time trial at Praia do Pedrógão, granfondo/mediofondo in Pombal. Whether this format carries over to the 2027 edition is unconfirmed.

history.origin_story: This is a new host-region brand (Pombal/Leiria), not a new event: the UCI officially relocated and rebranded Granfondo Coimbra Region (running since 2021) to Leiria for 2026, calling it 'the new name for the successful Granfondo Coimbra Region.' No Leiria-branded edition has actually been held yet — the planned 2026 debut was cancelled and postponed to spring 2027 due to storm damage. If founded is meant to track the underlying event lineage rather than the current brand, the real founding year is 2021 (see gran-fondo-coimbra.json).

biased_opinion.verdict: Real UCI Lineage, Zero Editions Actually Held
biased_opinion.summary: Granfondo Leiria Region – Sicó Lands is announced as a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier, successor to Granfondo Coimbra Region — but no edition of it has ever actually run. The UCI relocated and rebranded Coimbra (which ran five editions, 2021-2025) to Leiria/Pombal for 2026, with the same organizer, Cabreira Solutions, carrying it forward. Before it could debut, Storm Kristin caused widespread damage across the Leiria region — power outages, housing damage, closed hospitality businesses — and the organizing committee cancelled the planned March 28-29, 2026 event outright, postponing it to spring 2027 (reported by the UCI series site). As of this writing there is no confirmed 2027 date; a third-party calendar's March 2027 listing is explicitly marked unconfirmed. Treat this as an unproven successor to a real predecessor, not as an active race you can currently register for.
strengths: Real UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier status, inherited directly from the Granfondo Coimbra Region lineage (five editions, 2021-2025)
Same organizer (Cabreira Solutions) carrying continuity forward rather than starting from nothing
Multi-event format planned: a flat time trial plus granfondo/mediofondo road races into the Serra de Sicó limestone hills
weaknesses: Zero editions actually held under the Leiria name — the entire course and format picture above is a cancelled organizer plan, not race-day fact; field_size is null because no field has ever assembled
The planned 2026 debut was cancelled outright due to Storm Kristin damage, not merely rescheduled within the year
No confirmed 2027 date as of this writing — only an unconfirmed third-party calendar listing exists
Course figures (146 km/1,478 m) could change before an actual 2027 edition runs; treat them as provisional
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimate UCI-series successor to a real predecessor race, but don't book travel around it yet — nothing has actually run under this name, and the next date isn't confirmed.

final_verdict.one_liner: UCI qualifier with real lineage, but the 2026 debut was cancelled after storm damage and no confirmed date exists yet.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Not yet — no edition has run. Watch for an official 2027 date from the UCI Gran Fondo World Series or Cabreira Solutions before planning around it.
final_verdict.alternatives: For the same UCI-series lineage's real, held editions, see gran-fondo-coimbra.json (2021-2025, now discontinued). For an active Portuguese road gran fondo, see douro-gran-fondo.json (Peso da Régua, Douro Valley).

eligibility.notes: CONFIRMED and refined this wave (curl-verified 200, 2026-07-24). The planned March 28-29, 2026 debut (in Pombal, successor to gran-fondo-coimbra.json) was officially cancelled by the UCI on Feb 20 2026 due to Storm Kristin damage across the Leiria region, and postponed to spring 2027 — corroborated independently by the organizer's own live page ('Evento Adiado para 2027'), Gran Fondo Guide, and Jornal de Leiria local press. No confirmed 2027 date exists yet; a third-party (Gran Fondo Guide) listing shows March 27-28 2027 but is explicitly labeled 'Unconfirmed' with stale distance data — do not treat it as official. Corrected this wave: distance_km 148.1->146.0, elevation_m 1981->1478 (the organizer's own officially-announced, still-cancelled 2026 course plan, not the invented 6,500ft figure previously on file); date changed from '2026: March 29' (a date the event was never actually held on) to '2027: Spring TBD'. Course_description's prior claims about a Pombal fortress finish and a Leiria-to-Praia-do-Pedrogao time trial were revised/softened — the time trial is confirmed to start/finish at Praia do Pedrogao itself, and no source supports a dramatic fortress-finish sprint. Via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research, editorial wave 5, cross-checked against UCI official announcements, organizer page, and two independent Portuguese press sources.
